question: [Inner:] There is just so much on my mind.
[Inner:] A new school...
[Inner:] The move...
[Inner:]Everything that happened...
[Inner:] The exhaustion really isn't helping my nerves.
[Inner:] My legs are so sore...
[Inner:] What did mom always say... stretch before you think?
[Inner:] Something like that.
[Inner:] It's heavier than I remember...
[Inner:] The bus should be coming soon.
[Inner:] My legs feel so heavy. I wish I didn't have to go downstairs.
[Inner:] We've been here a few days now, and I still miss our old home.
[Inner:] I get why we had to move. And part of me is glad we did.
[Inner:] I just miss that familiarity...
[Inner:] Now I have to get used to everything again...
[Inner:] To a new house, a new school, a new life
[Inner:] It makes my head hurt
[Inner:] Do I really have to go?
[Inner:] I don't want to go... I really don't want to go...
[Inner:] I don't want a repeat of previous year...
[Inner:] I can still feel my heart pounding in my chest, but I *need* to go.
[Inner:] I can hear the bus in the distance. Let's just go before I start overthinking
[Inner:] I can't do it...
[Inner:] My head hurts and my heart feels like it is going to jump out of my chest.
[Inner:] I'll stay home today...I just hope mom understands.
[Inner:] My teacher looks friendly at least.
[Teacher]: Ah, welcome. You must be the transfer student I was informed about.
[You:] Uh, yes. That is me
[Teacher]: Excellent. We are glad to have you. You can call me Mr Brigsby.
[Teacher]: I'm starting the class soon. Go ahead and find a place to sit.
[Inner:] Find a seat... well, there is only one.
[Inner:] Luckily, it is at the back of the class.
[Inner:] I won't have to worry about everyone looking at me...
[Inner:] ... Or worse, the teacher asking *me* a question.
[Inner:] Although... he still might just do that.
[Inner:] Maybe I am hidden well enough behind everyone else so he won't notice me
[Inner:] I can barely see the board... good.
[Inner:] That means Mr Brigsby is less likely to ask me questions.
[Inner:] At least I can sit down now.
[Inner:] The bus ride wasn't exactly relaxing.
[Inner:] And I feel all wobbly.
[Inner:] What did he say about the powerhouse of the cell again?
[Inner:] It's so hard to focus.
[Inner:] He's talking so fast.
[Inner:] My mind feels like it is racing from left to right and back.
[Inner:] Maybe this was a bad idea...
[Inner:] I should have stayed home.
[Emily/Emiel:] Hey, are you okay?
[You:]Huh...sorry. Are you talking to me?
[Emily/Emiel:] Of course i'm talking to you, silly.
[Emily/Emiel:] You are the only one on my right after all.
[You:] That is...true. Yeah, you're right. That was a stupid question.
[Emily/Emiel:] It's not a stupid question.
[Emily/Emiel:] I can tell you are nervous.
[Emily/Emiel:] I didn't call you silly because I think you are actually silly.
[You:] Oh...
[Emily/Emiel:] You are new here right?
[You:] Um...yeah. I just moved here.
[Emily/Emiel:] No wonder you are nervous then.
[Emily/Emiel:] That's the first day jitters talking inside your head.
[You:] First day jitters... ?
[Emily/Emiel:] Wobbly legs, teeth clenching, avoiding the teachers gaze; really, really wanting to go home.
[Emily/Emiel:] Am I close?
[Inner:] Is it that obvious...?
[You:] Yeah..., yeah it is. How... could you tell?
[Emily/Emiel:] I recognized it from experience.
[Emily/Emiel:] I remember first sitting down, and feeling like the classroom would cave in.
[Emily/Emiel:] When Mr. Brigsby asked me a question about biology, I almost blurted out '24'.
[Emily/Emiel:] And don't get me started when I had to talk to others.
[Emily/Emiel:] I don't think I would have had the courage to talk to you, for example.
[Emily/Emiel:] Listen, I know it feels like a lot right now.
[Emily/Emiel:] But it gets better. Don't worry.

choices.text: [Inner:] Me? Someone needs courage to talk to *me*?!
[Inner:] Someone with this much confidence knows what it is like?

question: [Inner:] Me? Someone needs courage to talk to *me*?!
[Inner:] Do I look that scary?
[You:] Courage? To talk to me? I don't fully understand.
[Emily/Emiel:] Well, you are kind of cute.
[Emily/Emiel:] And like I said before, I used to be a nervous wreck.
[Emily/Emiel:] Point is, I've been in your shoes at one point too.
[You:] Oh...um. Thank you... I needed that.
[Inner:] Someone this confident knows what it is like?
[Inner:] Guess I never really thought about if others felt like that too...
[Inner:] Even you? I find that hard to imagine to be honest.
[Emily/Emiel:] Even me! And a lot of other people too.
[Emily/Emiel:] School is a frightening place after all.
[Emily/Emiel:] But, don't let that define you.
[You:] I — Thank you... I needed that.
[Emily/Emiel:] You're welcome. You feel a bit better?
[You:] A little, yeah.
[Inner:] I can think a bit more clearly now, at least.
[Inner:] I want to thank them, but my throat feels dry...
[Inner:] I want to thank them, but my throat feels dry...

choices.text: [You:] Thank you, again. I'm glad you talked to someone like me.
[You:] Thank you, again. It feels reassuring to know that someone knows what it is like.

question: [Emily/Emiel:] I'm glad you are feeling better.
[Emily/Emiel:] I always try to help out when I can.
[Emily/Emiel:] And know that it gets easier to connect to people.
[Emily/Emiel:] Just like how we are connecting right now, see? Easy, right?
[You:] I... I have a bit of trouble understanding it fully. But I think I get it.
[Emily/Emiel:] I'm glad you are feeling better.
[Emily/Emiel:] Overthinking everything is why the first day of school seems so overwhelming.
[Emily/Emiel:] But really it goes by quite fast if you just keep your head cool.
[Emily/Emiel:] Doesn't your head feel lighter now that you can focus on this conversation?
[You:] A little bit, I think...
[Emily/Emiel:] Oh, seems Mr. Brigsby is about to go on a lecture again.
[Emily/Emiel:] Why don't we talk after class?
[You:] Okay... I think I can do that.
[Inner:] I hope I can at least.
Hey... I'm glad you waited.
Of course. I said I would, didn't I?
This is a lot harder than I thought... Take a deep breath. You can do this.
How are you feeling?
Still overwhelmed, but, not as much as I thought.
Glad to hear it. Means I made the right choice.
For now. Take a deep breath in—
—And out.
In—
If you ever feel nervous again, focus on your breathing.
If that doesn't help, I am always nearby.
You'll be just fine, I know you will.
Deep breaths... and ask for help.
I can do that. Breathing is easy at least... Right?
[Inner:] I should ask them about it.

choices.text: [You:] Um. I wanted to ask about a feeling I've been having.
[You:] Um. I wanted to ask about how to become more like you.

question: Um. I wanted to ask about a feeling I've been having.
A feeling? What kind.
You mentioned having had trouble talking to others.
After our talk during class I started thinking.
This is a bit awkward...
But, is it normal to feel *different* than others?
Ah-
I think I understand.
Of course it is.
Different isn't always bad, I'd even argue It's good. Usually, that is.
If everyone was the same or felt the same, things would be so boring.
Can you imagine a world full of Mr. Brigsby's? *Horrifying*, I know.
Jokes aside. I know what you mean.
I'm going to sound a little sappy with this, but—
—You feeling different from others is totally normal and okay. A lot of people feel that way.
But, I understand if that takes a bit to process. Think about, but, rest first. Okay?
Yeah... that is a good idea.
Thank you.
Um. I wanted to ask about how to become more like you.
Like me?
Ah.
